step1Profile.title: Your restaurant
step1Profile.subtitle: A few details to get started.
step1Profile.postalDetected: Postal code {{code}} — school zone and public holidays detected automatically.
step1Profile.postalHint: Enter a 5-digit postal code to automatically detect the school holidays zone.
step1Profile.siretHint: Required for DPAE filings and contracts. You can add it later from Preferences > Profile.
step1Profile.botLocaleHint: Bot currently French-only — other languages coming soon.

step2Subroles.title: Team sub-roles
step2Subroles.subtitle: Select the positions present in your restaurant. The default HCR level is suggested — you can adjust it per employee.

step3Services.title: Your services
step3Services.subtitle: What service rhythm? This choice creates the initial template — you can refine it later.

midi-soir.label: Lunch + Dinner
midi-soir.desc: Two distinct services on the same day.

coupure.label: Split shift
coupure.desc: A day in two parts separated by a long break (e.g. 11h–15h then 18h–23h).

custom.label: Custom
custom.desc: Design your services in the visual editor — you'll come back here afterwards.

errors.noOpenDays: Select at least one open day
errors.noStaff: Indicate at least one headcount (kitchen or floor)

step4Employees.title: Your team
step4Employees.subtitle: Add your first employees. Only name, phone and position are required to start — contract, IBAN and contact details can be filled in later.
step4Employees.missingSideWarn: No employees in {{section}} — you can add them later from the team page.

stepStyle.title: Your team logic
stepStyle.subtitle: How auto-staffing balances stability, payroll and flexibility. Editable any time from Preferences › Rule.

styleDescriptions.equilibre: Distributes effort evenly. Fills contractual hours before opening overtime.
styleDescriptions.equipe-stable: Favours the same employees on the same services week after week.
styleDescriptions.economique: Avoids overtime — the most direct lever on payroll.
styleDescriptions.resilience: Distributes hours evenly — no one is overloaded and everyone can absorb a surprise.

overtime.title: Overtime
overtime.intro: Legal overtime cap and distribution rule between employees. HCR convention: threshold at 39h/week.

controlled.label: Controlled
controlled.desc: Up to a weekly cap that you set

overtime.capExplanation39: No overtime — same as strict mode.
overtime.capExplanation110: Up to {{count}}h of OT at 110% per employee per week.
overtime.capExplanation120: Up to 4h at 110% + {{count}}h at 120% per employee per week.
overtime.capExplanationMax: Up to 4h at 110% + 4h at 120% + 1h at 150% per employee per week. Legal maximum.
overtime.distributionIntro: When auto-staffing needs OT, who receives it first.

willing-first.label: Volunteers first
willing-first.desc: Volunteer employees receive OT first, then by priority

by-priority.label: By priority
by-priority.desc: P1 receives OT first, regardless of preferences

even.label: Even distribution
even.desc: Distribute OT evenly among eligible employees

tapInOut.label: Time tracking
tapInOut.adminConfirmDesc: On each clock event, WhatsApp asks the manager to confirm. The clock event is recorded but marked pending.
tapInOut.syncDesc: Clocked hours replace planned hours in the monthly summary — payroll reflects hours actually worked.
tapInOut.latenessDesc: The monthly summary stays based on the schedule. Lateness and early departures are just listed on the employee profile (and in the monthly email).
tapInOut.earlyDesc: If an employee clocks in before the scheduled time, early minutes add up to paid hours. Otherwise, only the clock-out counts — hours start at the scheduled time.

workerPreferences.label: Employee preferences
workerPreferences.desc: Employees can indicate their availability and preferences.

compliance.title: Compliance — Labour law
compliance.intro: HCR convention. Unchecking a rule disables it for the planner — leave everything checked if unsure.

stepWhatsApp.title: WhatsApp assistant
stepWhatsApp.subtitle: Send Bernardo a WhatsApp message to check that your number is recognized.
stepWhatsApp.connectBodyReady: Open WhatsApp with the button below, send the pre-filled message, then check that Bernardo replies.
stepWhatsApp.connectBodyMissing: Bernardo's WhatsApp number is not configured in this environment. Add the WhatsApp link to send the test message.
stepWhatsApp.phoneFallbackBody: From your phone, open WhatsApp and send this message to Bernardo's number.
stepWhatsApp.pairingBody: Bernardo recognizes each person from the number saved in Comptoir. If an employee uses another number, update their profile before inviting them.
stepWhatsApp.adminPhoneHelp: Use the same number you will use to message Bernardo.
stepWhatsApp.remindersBody: Choose how often planning reminders are sent by WhatsApp. You can change this later in Preferences → Rules.

reminderOptions.dailyDesc: A reminder the evening before for next-day services.
reminderOptions.weeklyDesc: A summary of the week on Sunday evening.

whatWorks.schedule: Answer questions about the published schedule.
whatWorks.replacements: Receive unavailability reports and help find a replacement.
whatWorks.timeclock: Handle clock-in/out if you enable it in the advanced rule.

step5Finish.title: You're ready
step5Finish.subtitle: Summary of your setup. You can change everything later from Preferences and Team.

after.schedule: Build your first week on /schedule, or let auto-staffing propose a plan.
after.preferences: Tweak service and sub-role details from /preferences.
after.staff: Complete employee profiles (address, IBAN, contracts) on /staff.
after.whatsapp: WhatsApp remains optional: if you did not test it now, add the link later.
